title: MTDL allocates IDR 120 billion for logistics centre expansion in Cibitung
body: PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL) has budgeted capital expenditure of IDR 120 billion for the expansion of logistics centre in Cibitung, Bekasi.

title: SMLE secures IDR 43 billion loan for capex
body: PT Sinergi Multi Lestarindo Tbk (SMLE) has secured a facility of IDR 43 billion in order to boost its capital expenditure (capex).

title: Unusual stock price surge, stock exchange authority suspends MLPT
body: The authority of Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) has suspended the transaction of PT Multipolar Technology Tbk (MLPT) after showing an unusual rapid price surge.

title: August 2024 saw trade surplus dropped 6.96% yoy
body: Indonesia’s trade surplus in August 2024 of USD 2.89 billion dropped 6.96% year-on-year (yoy) from USD 3.11 billion seen in August 2023.

title: Morning session, JCI fluctuated at 7,820
body: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fluctuated at over 7,820 since the opening bell today (17/9) until 11 AM WIB. Arriving at over 7,800, JCI broke its highest record, after lounging around 7,200-7,500 for a while.

title: MTDL allocates IDR 120 billion for logistics centre expansion in Cibitung
body: PT Metrodata Electronics Tbk (MTDL) has budgeted capital expenditure of IDR 120 billion for the expansion of logistics centre in Cibitung, Bekasi.

title: SMLE secures IDR 43 billion loan for capex
body: PT Sinergi Multi Lestarindo Tbk (SMLE) has secured a facility of IDR 43 billion in order to boost its capital expenditure (capex).

title: Unusual stock price surge, stock exchange authority suspends MLPT
body: The authority of Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) has suspended the transaction of PT Multipolar Technology Tbk (MLPT) after showing an unusual rapid price surge.

title: August 2024 saw trade surplus dropped 6.96% yoy
body: Indonesia’s trade surplus in August 2024 of USD 2.89 billion dropped 6.96% year-on-year (yoy) from USD 3.11 billion seen in August 2023.

title: Morning session, JCI fluctuated at 7,820
body: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fluctuated at over 7,820 since the opening bell today (17/9) until 11 AM WIB. Arriving at over 7,800, JCI broke its highest record, after lounging around 7,200-7,500 for a while.

title: Indonesian gov’t to aim for IDR 22 trillion in next week’s SUN auction
body: The Indonesian government will hold another round of auction of Government Securities (SUN) next Tuesday (17/9) with an indicative target set to IDR 22 trillion.

title: MAHA to allocate IDR 200 billion for buyback
body: PT Mandiri Herindo Adiperkasa Tbk (MAHA) is allocating IDR 200 billion in order to repurchase shares (buyback) of a maximum 20% of its paid-up capital.

title: KDSI to set up stock split with ratio of 1:4
body: PT Kedawung Setia Industrial Tbk (KDSI) is preparing to have a stock split with a ratio of 1:4, meaning that one old share will be divided into 4 shares with new value.

title: PMJS to form new subsidiary through DIPO, investing IDR 155.99 billion
body: PT Putra Mandiri Jembar Tbk (PMJS), through its subsidiary, PT Dipo Internasional Pahala Otomotif (DIPO), has established a new subsidiary called PT Mitdi Teja Otomotif (MTO).

title: Fourteen million shares of Panca Global (PEGE) released by Anugrah Inti Karisma
body: On Wednesday, September 11, the selling of over 14.03 million shares of PT Panca Global Kapital Tbk (PEGE) by PT Anugerah Inti Karisma set the tone for the market hints. After this most recent transaction, the investor's share portion of the issuer's shares—which are in the securities trading intermediary sector—was reduced to 15.84% from approximately 16.34%.

title: 2.72 billion shares sold by foreigners, net foreign recorded at -386.84 million shares
body: On Wednesday, September 11, foreign investors sold a total of 2.72 billion shares. The foreign buying volume was just about 2.34 billion shares, therefore net foreign volume closed at -386.84 million shares. PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL), a domestic and foreign shipping services company, ranked first in terms of purchase volume with 47.01 million shares and a net volume of 43.96 million shares.

title: GDST confident with sales target of IDR 2.5 trillion
body: PT Gunawan Dianjaya Steel Tbk (GDST) is optimistic of being able to score sales of IDR 2.5 trillion at the end of 2024, relatively stable compared to 2023 sales.

title: Expanding factory in Cilegon, Nippon Shokubai Indonesia readies IDR 1.69 trillion
body: Nippon Shokubai Indonesia, a petrochemical company that has been investing in Indonesia since 1996, is preparing IDR 1.69 trillion for factory expansion in Cilegon.

title: Dima Investindo to gain control over 55% of KMDS
body: PT Dima Investindo (DI) will acquire 55% of PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDS) owned by PT Miki Ojisan Indomitra (MOI). DI is not directly or indirectly affiliated with KMDS.

title: Waskita Toll Road lend IDR 270 billion for Cimanggis-Cibitung Toll Road
body: PT Waskita Toll Road (WTR), a subsidiary of PT Waskita Karya (Persero) Tbk (WSKT), has channelled a loan facility worth IDR 270 billion to PT Cimanggis Cibitung Tollways (CCT).

title: MSIN now has control over RAAM's shares
body: PT MNC Digital Entertainment Tbk (MSIN) steps in as the new shareholder of PT Tripar Multivision Tbk (RAAM) via private placement of 619.42 million shares, bought for IDR 500 each.

title: PGAS to pair up with InJourney’s subsidiary for tourism industry
body: PT Perusahaan Gas Negara Tbk (PGAS) has reportedly collaborated with PT ITDC Nusantara Utilitas (INU), a subsidiary of InJourney Tourism Development Corporation, to develop a gas network in tourism sector.

title: Accelerating BSD City innovation, Living Lab Ventures to launch International Landing Pad
body: Living Lab Ventures (LLV), corporate venture capital (VC) under Sinar Mas Lang, collaborates with the government of New South Wales (NSW), Australia, to introduce International Landing Pad in order to boost start-ups and companies from both countries to launch global expansion.

title: Investors' share portions in Perma Plasindo (BINO) still decreasing
body: Two significant investors in PT Perma Plasindo Tbk (BINO) saw a decrease in share portions on Tuesday, September 10, following a similar decline on the previous trading day. Foreign investor Ruhong Holding Pte Ltd, which possesses over 1.94 billion BINO shares, currently has only approximately 89.01% ownership in the company. Furthermore, the owner of 174 million shares, PT Intan Pariwara, saw a minor decrease to 7.94%.

title: JCI stable at the green zone all day
body: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) did not budge from the green zone all day today (12/9). It was opened and closed at the same level, 7,798.

title: Two Sumber Energi Andalan shareholders continue to buy and sell shares
body: Two key investors in PT Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA), an issuer providing consulting services to the mining and energy industries, continue to trade the company's shares. Foreign investor Zurich Assets International Ltd decided to sell 17.25 million shares of ITMA on Monday, September 9, reducing its ownership to 5.57%. On the other hand, PT Fersindo Nusa Jaya's share portion rose to 6.82% after it added 2.84 million shares through four securities.

title: RGAS and PGAS to work on gas network project worth IDR 29.5 billion
body: PT Kian Santang Muliatama Tbk (RGAS) and PT Perusahaan Gas Negara Tbk (PGAS) have collaborated to work on gas network construction project with a contract value of ID 29.5 billion.

title: JSMR owns 26% of shares in Bogor-Serpong Toll Road operator
body: PT Jasa Marga (Persero) Tbk (JSMR) made capital investment in PT Bogor Serpong Infra Selaras (BSIS), a joint venture managing Bogor-Serpong via Parung Toll Road.

title: Vice Minister of Religion: High-quality human resources required for sharia economic centre
body: There is a need for capable and reliable human resources to boost Indonesia as sharia economic centre.

title: BTON will distribute cash dividend of IDR 10 per share
body: PT Betonjaya Manunggal Tbk (BTON) will schedule a cash dividend payment of IDR 7.2 billion or IDR 10 per share.

title: IDXESGL welcomes 3 issuers as new constituents
body: Three issuers are now listed as constituents of IDX ESG Leaders (IDXESGL) following a major evaluation in September 2024.

title: SMDM’s net profit increased threefold in the first half
body: PT Suryamas Dutamakmur Tbk (SMDM), a real estate management and developer, booked net profit of IDR 109.76 billion in the first half (H1) of 2024.

title: Managing government’s mining concession, Muhammadiyah to form 2 companies
body: Muhammadiyah Central Board, one of the major religion organisations in Indonesia, has formed two companies to manage mining concessions granted by the Indonesian government.

title: EAST to share interim dividend of IDR 5.15 billion
body: PT Eastparc Hotel Tbk (EAST) will distribute interim dividend of IDR 5.15 billion for the fiscal year (FY) 2024, down 30.55% from interim dividend for FY 2023 of IDR 7.42 billion.

title: A new building in progress, LTLS to remain committed to sustainability through subsidiaries
body: PT Lautan Luas Tbk (LTLS), a chemical distributor, implements sustainability strategies through two of its subsidiaries, PT Lautan Natur Krimerindo (LNK), and another subsidiary engaged in the supporting services sector.

title: BEKS to set up rights issue of 5.18 series C shares
body: PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk (BEKS) is planning a rights issue of 5.18 billion series C shares of IDR 50 per share.

title: HEXA to expand to Foton vehicle reparation business
body: PT Hexindo Adiperkasa Tbk (HEXA) will expand its business reach to reparation services for Foton cars and trucks, a Chinese vehicle brand, starting this year.

title: Diversifying, Adaro to divest thermal coal business
body: PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO) plans to divest portions in one of its subsidiaries engaged in thermal coal mining business, PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia (AAI).

title: Focusing on digital business, Krom Bank Indonesia to divest assets for IDR 2.95 billion
body: PT Krom Bank Indonesia Tbk (BBSI) has sold its assets in Bandung worth IDR 2.95 billion.

title: Buana Finance to secure credit facility of IDR 300 billion from Bank HSBC
body: PT Buana Finance Tbk (BBLD) has signed a credit facility agreement with PT Bank HSBC Indonesia.
